world.title: ETHIOPIA: Monsignor Medhin Tesfa, Appointed Coadjutor Apostolic Vicar of Gambella, Ethiopia
world.description:
By Bezawit Assefa
On  Saturday, March 21, the Holy See Press Office announced that the Holy Father had appointed Rev. Hailemariam Medhin Tesfay, SDB, as Coadjutor Apostolic Vicar of the Apostolic Vicariate of Gambella, Ethiopia.
Rev. Hailemariam Medhin Tesfay was born on 1 October 1973 in Alitena, Ethiopia. A member of the Salesians of Don Bosco, he made his perpetual vows in Addis Ababa in 2001 and was ordained a priest on 4 May 2003 in Adigrat.
Following his ordination, he served in various pastoral and administrative roles, including vicar, parish priest, and bursar in Adwa. He later completed advanced theological studies at the Salesian Pontifical University in Rome, earning both a licentiate and a doctorate in theology.
His ministry included teaching theology at Utume, Kenya, and serving as rector and parish priest in Mekanissa, Addis Ababa. From 2019 to 2025, he was provincial superior of the Salesian Visitation “Kidane Mehret” for Ethiopia–Eritrea and concurrently served as president of the Conference of Major Religious Superiors in Ethiopia.
As Coadjutor Apostolic Vicar, Msgr. Hailemariam Medhin Tesfay will assist and, in due time, succeed Bishop Roberto Bergamaschi, SDB, the current Apostolic Vicar of Gambella.
The Catholic Bishops’ Conference of Ethiopia expressed its joy at the appointment and conveyed its heartfelt wishes for a blessed and fruitful ministry to Msgr. Hailemariam Medhin.

world.title: KENYA: Pope Appoints Friar Obed Muriungi, Auxiliary Bishop of Kenya’s Nairobi Archdiocese
world.description:
KCCB – Commission for Social Communications Team
Pope Leo XIV has appointed Monsignor Obed Muriungi Karobia, a member of the Order of Friars Minor Conventual (OFM Conv), as Auxiliary Bishop of the Catholic Archdiocese of Nairobi, strengthening apostolic leadership in one of Kenya’s most vibrant ecclesiastical jurisdictions.
The appointment was officially announced in Rome on Thursday, March 26. It was formally communicated to the Kenyan Bishops through the Apostolic Nuncio to Kenya, Archbishop Bert Van Megen.
Auxiliary Bishop-elect Karobia brings to the episcopacy a rich heritage of Franciscan spirituality grounded in humility, fraternity, and service.
Born on June 29, 1979, in Meru, Kenya, Bishop-elect Karobia has journeyed through a steady path of religious formation and priestly ministry. He made his simple profession in 2004 and his solemn profession in 2010 before being ordained a priest in 2012. Over the years, he has distinguished himself through dedicated pastoral service, leadership within the Franciscan fraternity, and a deep commitment to evangelization.
Notably, in 2019, he was elected the first Minister Provincial of the Province of St. Francis in Kenya. As the Auxiliary Bishop-elect Karobia will support the Archbishop in shepherding the faithful, enhancing pastoral outreach, and strengthening ecclesial structures.
Until his appointment, he had been serving as the Supreme Moderator of the Franciscan Servants of Mary Queen of Love Fraternity, a religious community of priests and brothers.

science.title: Implantable 'living pharmacy' produces multiple drugs inside the body
science.description: A multi-institutional team of scientists, co-led by Northwestern University, has taken a crucial step toward implantable "living pharmacies"—tiny devices containing engineered cells that continuously produce medicines inside the body. In a new study published in Device, the team engineered cells to simultaneously produce three different biologics—an anti-HIV antibody, a GLP-1-like peptide used to treat type 2 diabetes, and leptin, a hormone that regulates appetite and metabolism. When implanted under the skin of a small animal model, the device kept drug-producing cells alive and stably delivered all three therapies at once.

science.title: Hubble image: IC 486-where spiral arms and star formation meet
science.description: A luminous swirl set against the deep black of space, the barred spiral galaxy IC 486 glows with a soft, ethereal light in this new NASA/ESA Hubble Space Telescope Picture of the Month image. IC 486 lies right on the edge of the constellation Gemini (the Twins), about 380 million light-years from Earth. Classified as a barred spiral galaxy, it features a bright central bar-shaped structure from which its spiral arms unfurl, wrapping around the core in a smooth, almost ring-like pattern.
